seo_title: Chain-of-Thought Disrupts Simple Steering of Refusal in Large…
meta_description: Researchers find that chain-of-thought (CoT) traces in large reasoning models (LRMs) actively reinforce refusal, making them more robust against…
body_html:
Researchers have found that chain-of-thought (CoT) traces in large reasoning models (LRMs) actively reinforce refusal, making them more robust against activation-level interventions.

Key Findings

  • Activation steering reverses refusal in only 39% of cases when CoT is kept fixed, but removing CoT increases this to 70%
  • In a two-stage intervention where model regenerates its CoT under activation steering, refusal is reversed in 94% of cases
  • CoT can carry and reconstruct compliance signal independently

Robustness Against Activation-Level Interventions

This joint activation makes LRM more robust against activation-level interventions alone, but exposes CoT to a possible alternative surface attack.

Implications for Unlearning and Safety Evaluation

  • R-TOFU benchmark provides realistic CoT annotations and step-wise metrics that expose residual knowledge invisible to answer-level checks
  • Conventional answer-only objectives leave substantial forget traces in reasoning
target_keywords: Chain-of-Thought Disrupts Simple Steering of Refusal

source.title: Beyond a Single Direction: Chain-of-Thought Disrupts Simple Steering of Refusal
source.summary: Researchers have found that large reasoning models (LRMs) use chain-of-thought (CoT) traces to generate their outputs, which can complicate control mechanisms such as refusal. In one study, removing the CoT entirely increased the effectiveness of activation steering in reversing refusal from 39% to 70%. This suggests that the CoT actively reinforces refusal and can carry and reconstruct the compliance signal independently, making LRMs more robust against certain types of attacks. The findings highlight the importance of considering both residual stream activations and CoT when developing control mechanisms for LRM-based systems.
